rationale: DiffLOB is a credible research direction for regime-conditioned counterfactual LOB simulation, but current Torghut contracts and infrastructure are insufficient for direct production integration. The paper's empirical scope is narrow and does not support immediate live-trading promotion. A phased research-first implementation is viable if full LOB data contracts and offline diffusion pipelines are added with deterministic artifact governance.
rejection_reasons: Reject immediate production rollout: current repository runtime does not expose the full-depth LOB trajectory contract required by the method.
Reject immediate in-service model deployment: diffusion training/inference infrastructure is not present in the current Torghut service profile.
Reject direct policy coupling without additional evidence: paper evaluation window/universe is too limited for robust external validity.
recommendations: Implement a deterministic DiffLOB artifact contract first (hash-anchored manifest, seed, snapshot lineage, metric bundle).
Run diffusion training and counterfactual generation offline, then ingest outputs as read-only evidence artifacts.
Add walk-forward, stress-regime, and out-of-sample gates aligned to Torghut promotion policy before any execution-policy integration.
Keep the feature in simulation and paper-trading contexts until robustness thresholds pass repeatedly.
